title: Recall Coords
description: Save and recall important coordinates, including death locations, to easily find your way with Recall Coords
body:
# Recall Coords 

Recall Coords is a lightweight client-side coordinate saver and waypoint utility mod that helps you quickly save, organize, and recall important locations in your Minecraft worlds and multiplayer servers.

Perfect for:

*   Bases
*   Nether portals
*   Villages
*   Structures
*   Death locations
*   Exploration routes

✔ Client-side only  
✔ Works on most multiplayer servers  
✔ Automatic death coordinate saving  
✔ Lightweight and fast  
✔ Multi-dimension support  
✔ Simple `.rc` commands

***

## Features

- **Save coordinates** with custom names and descriptions
- **World specific storage** - each server/world maintains its own coordinate list
- **Multi dimensional support** (Overworld, Nether, End)
- **Automatic biome detection** - coordinates show the biome they were saved in
- **Death location tracking** - automatically saves where you died
- **Persistent storage** - coordinates are saved locally between game sessions
- **Interactive UI** with clickable coordinate names and hover tooltips
- **Copy coordinates** - click coordinates to copy them to clipboard

## Dependencies

### **FABRIC API REQUIRED**

This mod requires Fabric API to function properly. Make sure to install Fabric API alongside this mod. [Click To Download](https://modrinth.com/mod/fabric-api)

## Commands

**All commands use `.rc` (dot rc) instead of `/rc`**

### Adding Coordinates
- `.rc add auto <name> [description]`  - Auto detect current location & biome
- `.rc add <x> <y> <z> <name> [description]`  - Manually specify coordinates

### Managing Coordinates
- `.rc delete <name>`  - Delete a saved coordinate
- `.rc view <name>`  - View detailed information about a coordinate
- `.rc list`  - List all saved coordinates (clickable names!)
- `.rc death`  - View your last death location in current world
- `.rc help`  - Show all available commands

### Interactive Features
- **Click coordinate names** in `.rc list` to view details
- **Click coordinates** in `.rc view` and `.rc death` to copy to clipboard
- **Hover over elements** to see helpful tooltips
